title: Slugs, Permalinks, Redirects, Canonicals, and Route Manifests · Language Clinic: Permalink Policy
summary: Titles become unstable routes, Unicode collapses into empty slugs, case-only paths collide on deployment, old links vanish after rename, and canonical URLs point at preview origins. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the permalink policy increment in collision-free versioned route and redirect manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Select explicit or derived permalinks, trailing-slash convention, date behavior, language prefix, and base path with migration evidence.
Define Unicode normalization, transliteration or preservation, case, separators, reserved names, empties, length, and collision behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-slugs-permalinks-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-slugs-permalinks-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_slugs_permalinks_3(documents: int = 11, routes: int = 11, redirects: int = 3, collisions: int = 0, redirect_loops: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if documents < 1 or routes < 0:
        return False, routes, "route-count"
    if routes != documents:
        return False, routes, "route-coverage"
    if redirects < 0 or collisions > 0:
        return False, collisions, "route-collision"
    if redirect_loops > 0:
        return False, redirect_loops, "redirect-loop"
    assert routes == documents
    return True, routes, "stable-route-manifest"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Permalink Policy evidence · Theory Permalink Policy
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-permalink-policy-ty0rcu" that uses case 5188 to produce stable collision-free routes, canonicals, and an acyclic redirect graph. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Permalink Policy into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Build Permalink Policy evidence · Theory Permalink Policy, keep Permalink Policy connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-permalink-policy-ty0rcu
# Competency: ssg-permalink-policy.
# Case 5188: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_permalink_policy_ty0rcu(documents: int = 11, routes: int = 11, redirects: int = 3, collisions: int = 0, redirect_loops: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ty0rcu = "ty0rcu-ucr0yt-5188"
    assert evidence_variant_ty0rcu
    evidence_axes_ty0rcu = (
        "readerty0rcu",
        "source5188",
        "parseucr0yt",
        "routety0rcu5188",
        "artifact5188ty0rcu",
        "recoveryucr0yt5188",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ty0rcu)) == 6
    if documents < 1 or routes < 0:
        return False, routes, "route-count"
    if routes != documents:
        return False, routes, "route-coverage"
    if redirects < 0 or collisions > 0:
        return False, collisions, "route-collision"
    if redirect_loops > 0:
        return False, redirect_loops, "redirect-loop"
    assert routes == documents
    return True, routes, "stable-route-manifest"
